columns.description: (Parcel Number) Pierce County parcel numbers consist of 10 digits. Unplatted property parcel numbers are designated by Range, Township and Section numbers based on the property location, i.e., Parcel 0419211036: 04 = Range 04, 19 = Township 19, 21 = Section 21, Quarter Section = 1 (Northeast), lot number = 036. Platted property parcel numbers are designated by a 6 digit plat number, which preceeds the lot number, i.e., 709424 = Qunell's 4th Edition plat, 006-0 = Lot 6 of the plat.

columns.name: Site_Address
columns.description: (Site Address) This is the physical location address of the property. Site address may be different than the mailing address. When a site address contains "XXX" it means the house number is unverified. In the past, unverified house numbers were estimated using GIS (Geographic Information System). The estimated house number was discontinued because it was causing confusion as to whether the site address was correct or not. That is, some data users were assuming the "XXX" was a typo and using the estimated house number as if it was correct. For parcel Search see Parcel Search in Metadata Extensions in Metadata Reference Information Section

Depending on qualifications, a taxpayer may be exempt from "excess levies" (not counting emergency medical services levies), and may further be exempt from portions of the non-voter approved "regular levies". columns.description: (Use Code) - The code that represents present highest and best use of the property for appraisal purposes. (see field LU_DESC)

columns.name: Taxable_Value
columns.description: (Taxable Value) - The assessed value of the property, minus any exemptions granted to the property

columns.name: Improvement_Value
columns.description: (Improvement Value for 2015)The assessed values of the improvements. Improvements are defined as anything done to raw land with the intention of increasing its value. Thus a structure erected on the property constitutes one very common type of improvement, although other actions, such as those taken to improve drainage, are also improvements. This data includes values of subsequent records, but does not include mobile home values

columns.name: Land_Value
columns.description: (Land Value for 2015) The sum of the characteristics of the land, not including timberland. This value includes assessed values of land development features, but does not include any structural values. It also includes values of subsequent records associated with the parcel. Methods of valuation are: Site value: value assigned to a parcel of land in its entirety, without regard to square footage. As such, many parcels of land can be grouped together and valued similarly without regard to differences of size. Square feet/acreage: computation of the total area of the parcel into square feet. On a regular lot, width * depths. On an irregular lot, computation of total area. Front feet: value of the land is computed using front footage. This usually applies to waterfront property

columns.name: Business_Name
columns.description: (Business Name) A commercial property business name, or mobile home park name. The field is populated and maintained by the commercial appraisers. They will update the data if they are on-site and find a change is needed. Physically inspection are done once every 6 years. If a commercial appraiser finds out a business name has changed or is on site sooner than the 6 year inspection cycle, they will update the business name if needed. Business names are determined by the appraiser and may be set to be the parcel owner, rather than the name of a strip mall or a specific business

name: Floodplain Cross Sections
description: This is 2017 DFIRM Data for cross section lines. New to the DFIRM data is the coastal flood study, that adds Velocity Zones (VE) along Puget Sound and the Seclusion Boundary. Seclusion areas are located where floodplains are affected by non-accredited levees and retain 1970s modeled flood hazards. These lines show the locations of channel surveys used to calculate flood elevations in the hydraulic models. name: Park Access (County Aggregates)
description: This dataset shows the percentage of Pierce County residents who live near a park or designated open space and near a community or public recreation center. Near is defined as within 2 miles. The measure calculates combined access to these at the neighborhood (Census Block Group) level and weights the results by population to estimate the share of all residents with convenient access to parks or open space and community or public recreation centers.
